title: Shallow Copy Check
description: Predict nested sharing with spread and copy-on-write patterns.

questions.explanation: Object spread is shallow: `copy` is new, but `copy.address` and `user.address` still point to the same nested object.
Array spread is also shallow. The outer array is new, but elements inside are still shared references.

questions.prompt:
Which update **does not** mutate the original `user`?

```js
const user = { name: "Ana", address: { city: "SP" } };
```
questions.explanation: Copy-on-write replaces every nested level you change. Options b–d mutate shared nested data or the original object.

questions.prompt: What is true about `{ ...obj }` and `Object.assign({}, obj)`?

options.text: Both create a shallow copy of the top level only
Both create a deep copy of all nested objects
Spread is deep; Object.assign is shallow
Neither copies anything; both create aliases

questions.explanation: Spread and Object.assign copy enumerable own properties into a new object. Nested values are still shared references.

questions.prompt: Given `const state = { items: [{ id: 1, qty: 1 }] };`, which pattern correctly increments `qty` without mutating `state`?

options.text: Build `nextState` with spread at both the `items` array and the changed item object

questions.explanation: Shallow copy of `state` or `items` still shares inner item objects. You must copy the array and replace the changed item.
